title: 7 Stop Payment Requests for PADs
body_text:
7	Stop Payment Requests for PADs
7.1	Overview of Stop Payment Requests for PADs
If the Payor (the member) cancels a PAD and the Payee debits the Payor芒聙聶s account, the Payor can make a claim for reimbursement as described in Section 7.2, Handling Disputed PADs, in the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part.
The Payor can also request that the financial institution put a stop payment on the PAD if there is reason to believe that the Payee will try to debit the Payor芒聙聶s account again. However, the Payor is responsible for notifying the Payee of cancellation of the Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement. A stop payment request can be used in addition to cancelling a PAD, but it cannot be used in place of the Payor separately contacting the Payee for cancellation of their agreement.
If a stop payment is placed on a PAD, the debit will not be posted to the Payor芒聙聶s account. Instead, the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system will apply the stop payment to the PAD when it receives the file of incoming AFTs from Central 1. The financial institution must then return the PAD to the originating financial institution as code 903, Payment Stopped/Recalled. The return must be processed within one business day.
Stop payment requests are available if the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system facilitates stop payments for PADs. A stop payment request is only effective if the amount of the PAD corresponds to the amount recorded on the Stop Pre-authorized Debit Request, Form 3386. If the Payee issues a PAD for a different amount, the PAD will be debited to the Payor芒聙聶s account.
Related Information
Stop Pre-authorized Debit Request,
Form 3386
7.2	Requesting a Stop Payment on a PAD
Proceed as follows to process a stop payment request from the Payor (the member):
Complete the Stop Pre-authorized Debit Request, Form 3386. Completion of this form is self-explanatory. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Stop Pre-authorized Debit Request, Form 3386
Have the Payor sign the form.
Retain the form for your records.
Follow your internal procedures to enter the stop payment request on the banking system.
If the banking system identifies a PAD for which a stop payment request was issued, return the PAD to the originating financial institution as code 903, Payment Stopped/Recalled, within one business day. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures on returning AFT transactions.
Related Information
Stop Pre-authorized Debit Request,
Form 3386
END
